hypothesis: If a large language model is continually pre-trained on domain-specific chemical and MOF-related literature before supervised fine-tuning on MOF property datasets, then it will achieve lower prediction error (MAE) for MOF properties such as band gap and gas adsorption compared to models without domain-specific pre-training and to non-LLM baselines, because exposure to specialized chemical knowledge during pre-training enables the model to develop richer representations of MOF structural descriptors that transfer effectively to property prediction tasks, particularly in low-resource fine-tuning scenarios.
